text: Pokemon and I both turned 30. As I reflect on my personal journey I must voice my gratitude. Pokemon has always been my happy place. It's how I learned how to read. It taught me to be kind. It gave me common ground with members of my own family and was the foundation of my career in veterinary medicine and animal training. I will always love Pokemon. It is so much more than a franchise for me. It is a huge part of who I am and I don't know who I would be if I wasn't a Pokemon trainer. From the bottom of my heart, thank you 🙏

text:
Ironically, the slowed down shots that were specifically made for ai usage look even BETTER without it. The alternative (almost contemporary) visuals of Somi give a beautiful but disturbingly pleasant aesthetic. It let us viewers know that something is going on internally, and gives almost an intimate 1-on-1 perspective with Somi while she’s going through the motions.

There isn’t much going on while Somi contorts her body and you’re slightly waiting/testing new gens attention spans. Neutral colors and that song composition intro have us questioning the thematic in the best way, it adds that feeling of “waiting/wondering where the song structure is going, yet you know something’s about to happen.” Feels like anticipation for an inevitable jump scare mixed with awe for Somi’s visuals. Finally that bass drop with the sea anemone scene hits home!

It’s the feeling of encountering a real goddess/god based on scriptures, that build up feels straight up NUMINOUS!
author: @jiffysanchez

There isnt much going on while Somi contorts her body and youre slightly waiting/testing new gens attention spans. Neutral colors and that song composition intro have us questioning the thematic in the best way, it adds that feeling of waiting/wondering where the song structure is going, yet you know somethings about to happen. text:
Still blows my mind that to celebrate the 30th anniversary of the franchise they decided that the "Pokemon Walkman" was the best way to celebrate. When a Gameboy Mini with 2 cartridges one for Pokemon Red & one for Pokemon Blue would have been a great way for old-school fans of the franchise to get that nostalgia and show the kids of today how they used to play the game back in the day, after all a lot of people in their early thirties today have kids in the age span of about 6-10.

text:
Winds and Waves looks great! Still refuse to buy a switch 2 with the company in this state. The Switch "2" isn't a new console its just an update and I'm sick of the new ownership. The switch 2 is a massive disappointment, we have been waiting for a >>>NEW<<< console for YEARS and we get a bigger slab with magnets and the SAME SOULLESS UI we've had for years! Nintendo doesn't have a soul anymore and many aren't realizing that.

Remember the days when the people in the top positions of Nintendo would be the face of Nintendo? Everything was so rewarding and fun! Now there's no music anywhere, no fun loading screens, no homepage customization, no fun sounds, it all looks so grey and stripped apart. There's nothing fun! The UI team has to be insulted, I mean come on you know how creative and talented they are. You're telling me they just gave us a new select border and rounded edges?!?!

The face of Nintendo now is the price of their characters, and not a fun rewarding company that builds trust with their players. All hype and trust is behind such a massive pay wall. No money is not my personal issue, the issue it that Nintendo was know for being family friendly with their prices. $30 or $40 games. But $80-$100 GAMES?!?! Get real. This isn't the same company. They just know their fans will do anything and I'm sick of it. If you're going to charge this much. AT LEAST make the console feel fun.

(You know what, It just hit me. They don't want you to have a fun console. They wan't you to download the games specifically for it to feel fun. They don't wanna make a fun console. They just want you to get in (console on and at the eshop) and get out (playing the games). This is why I believe they're sticking with the same soulless console that we've had for 8 YEARS. Going on 9+ because guess what, It's just a Switch Pro. The same switch we've had but stronger, prices jacked way up, bigger, magnets, the UI looks ever so slightly different with rounded edges. And no, this isn't a NEW Nintendo Switch like the New 3ds and New 3ds Xl. There's a difference. One is forced apon (Switch 2), and one was not (New 3ds).

Sorry to break it to you it's not a "new console" it's the same thing but upscaled along with the price. Nintendo just wants your money.

text:
It's really weird for me especially because I never stopped playing it, a couple months off every year or so but just because of real life situations and changes and I've only been more active and healthier from it since then, able to more quickly lose any weight even when I regain it in the winter months, it's gradually come back up less and less and gone back down sooner and quicker. And I have had some really important moments from going outside with it, not even just with the game or even friends with it, but just being able to connect with people more easily, not being completely as shy and having social anxiety, and seeing all the little things in my neighborhood and where I've gone on all my travels that I had never seen before and never noticed because thanks to Pokémon Go, I have actually been able to enjoy the movement and transit, not just hurry to get to the destination. It's just as important to stop and slow down and breath the fresh air you never knew you so desperately needed and open your eyes wide to all the lights and colors of the world, hear the little things that whisper secrets and lead to you having brand new experiences and many moments to treasure forever. And again, all with that connect you make not just to the people around you that you share it with, but the planet and natural and your home itself! And it's beautiful! I've now memorized every single step and hidden grotto park and destroyed or damaged slab of sidewalk in my neighborhood now and know the fastest ways to get from on end to the other even when multiple ways are blocked by traffic or construction or fallen debris and such, and I've learned to memorize that for different far spread out pockets of different areas ranging from my own home state and beyond out of state.

And I'll be honest, I never would've been nearly as happy and free to actually move my own two legs and feel sunlight, rain, fog and wind, snow and sounds, bright and gentle colors and lights all around and still be able to slow down and relax and just enjoy the moment, however fleeting it is, that I'm alive and so thankful to be here. Because in American society at least, we are hyper conditioned to always rush to work ourselves to the bone and jeez no wonder we're all so miserable! That totally sucks and is the worst way to live your life! Because there's always more to life then just surviving! It's literally a journey! Just like Pokémon! How amazing and awesome is that!?! We are so lucky and happy to be here for that!😆

text:
@MichaelCorleone-u9u I can get being frustrated with the series, but taking it out on the series and on Nintendo in general in this way is honestly just, "What in the world are you thinking?" Please, find better, less accusatory ways of venting frustration and let adults enjoy thinking back on their fondest memories, especially on an anniversary. After all, it's not just Pokémon that plays to nostalgia. You know how obvious and common the nostalgia references are in the Mario series with how the 8-bit sprites still appear everywhere, right? That's why I pointed out Nintendo in general and not just Game Freak, and it doesn't mean at all that it's playing toward that very messed-up "preference" that you linked it to.
As well, what makes an idea "original" or "good?" Just because you don't like something doesn't make it outright bad. It's all in how well the idea does what it sets out to do, and just because you (subjectively) don't like it doesn't mean it (objectively) failed or that it's bad. It is fine for something to just not be to your personal preference. Especially because different people can still actually like it, and you should let them like it if that's the case. More power to them.
That is my problem and my frustration with the Pokémon fanbase and with the gaming fanbase in general. Everyone seems so quick to pick fights and call even their fellow fan out in asinine ways like calling them a "sellout," a "shill," or a "fanboy" just for liking or defending something that someone else doesn't. God forbid people have different opinions on such a diverse hobby. This isn't toward you in particular, but it's like, "Calm the heck down and let people enjoy things."

Just because you don't like something doesn't make it outright bad. It's all in how well the idea does what it sets out to do, and just because you (subjectively) don't like it doesn't mean it (objectively) failed or that it's bad. It is fine for something to just not be to your personal preference. Especially because different people can still actually like it, and you should let them like it if that's the case. More power to them.That is my problem and my frustration with the Pokmon fanbase and with the gaming fanbase in general. Everyone seems so quick to pick fights and call even their fellow fan out in asinine ways like calling them a "sellout," a "shill," or a "fanboy" just for liking or defending something that someone else doesn't. God forbid people have different opinions on such a diverse hobby. This isn't toward you in particular, but it's like, "Calm the heck down and let people enjoy things."

text: ​@GnollWitch777 Game development is very complicated, especially for AAA studios! Just because they are rich studios doesn't mean they don't have limitations. Plus, the Switch 2, even though it's identical to the Switch 1, probably needs certain modifications to optimize a game for it. Gamefreek may be a multi-billion dollar company, but they are also people. And considering it's Japan, I don't doubt that there are some issues with how the employees working on the games practically give their lives to make them. Working in Japan is much more life-destroying; some people literally die from overwork! That's why, as critical as I am of Gamefreek, like everyone else, I also recognize how things must be for people working in this industry in Japan. He must be hardworking. But that's just what I think is the case.

text:
@NateDellenbach Just like other people, I'm pretty impressed with the water quality, and that's very important for games set in a region with lots of water.

The models look good, but not many people have complained about the Switch 1/2 Pokémon models, since they have looked pretty good for a while now. Could be taken further, but it's good enough, that's for sure.

Now, the textures of the ground... Definitely don't give me the most confidence. They don't look great. Better than Scarlet and Violet, and better than ZA, but so it should. There was no reason to have lack lustre graphics in those games, and there is finally some improvements for Wind and Wave. But honestly... It's not filling me with confidence. It's still lacking, and I really, really hope the lazy PNG graphics are gone with the wind (pardon the pun), since ZA got a lot of criticism for that.

I have yet to be proven that Gamefreak wants that to change though, whatever the reason they've done it for Gen 8 and 9, I can't see a valid excuse for Switch 2 hardware, now that that is the focus going forward.

I'll buy the game, of course, I will love it, I know this, even with this flaw. I really do hope it's better than the trailer implies, since otherwise, the game is looking great. I would like to be proven wrong!

Edit: I'll add that the shading and lighting in the trailer look great too.

text:
Except these are still copy pasta graphics, but its not the core issue

I personally believe Arceus looked really good, its the gameplay loop that seems iffy. Then they took it even further backwards with ZA

The major complaint is how restrictive the games have become, despite millions of monsters available

Even here, if you want a game that emphasizes underwater you can play RSE. It got the job done for what the core battle experience has to offer

If you want a game that looks really good play Abzu or Sword of the Sea

Pokemon itself has always been about what you can catch and what you can do with them using the battle system. Raising them up under that structure using limited faculties like Vitamins and EV/IV min maxing

The rest of the experience is somewhat deferred. Meaning its exciting to encounter the legendary trio in the wild, but its not what you will be doing as the procession of the title

If however they were to switch up and make the game an MMORPG, the core loop will change. Meaning you might have to think about what kind of progress you are making alongside others in an online environment- which is the scope of most matchmaking titles (Fallguys, Battlefield, etc)

What I find most ironic is that most of what they seem to be marketing in the trailer is what you could find in Gen 8. And people actually felt that game regressed since Gen 7

I actually enjoyed 8 and felt that had they made the arena zones more dynamic (think sevii islands) it would have been far more compelling as a game. As of Gen 9 they wanted the expand their topography alongside the BotW craze. But its clear they should have outsourced

Again the problem seems like they are drifting in focus. They have all these spin offs and ambitious ideals for the future of the brand, but perhaps they should just emphasize what Champions has to offer and not try to be what other games are year by year

In my view they should use all these titles (ZA, Go!, Park, even Snap) as waypoints using Champions as the main hub. Essentially saying that where exclusives can be caught, they represent a different facet of setplay (i.e terastalizing, megas, zcrystals) that a person can perfect before squaring off in their field of strategy

Sort of a FFA to make playing the game yield different experiences. So far Winds/Waves doesn't merit anything beyond run of the mill obligatory release. Its the florida retirement center of modern Pokemon without the subtle charm of a next level release like GS was

I think by now we're tired of gimmicks, but certainly not aware of how a new game should proceed after Terastalization changed the landscape. Since ZA was SO restrictive, I'm not confident that the paramount answer is to take the series in the complete opposite direction. Its like saying that Pepsi lite didnt make much of an impact so instead overload the next one with sugar without first analysing what draws people to the original

text:
well, as long as neither of us is being mean to the other, we can have a discussion. I didn't even expect you to get ratioed like that because you're right, many people didn't like gen7 at the time.
I'm actually 28; my childhood games were dppt and hgss. Sun and Moon came out around when I was 18, so just post-childhood. So as for your evaluation of them sucking. I can acknowledge some of your points such as z moves being worse than mega evolution. absolutely! that was a downgrade. however, there were so many positive things about the region to me that I still really enjoyed it. having trials instead of gyms was definitely an ambitious/risky move and although it was arguably slightly messy in execution, I really liked that they tried something new. you make a good point that they really did. just bring gyms right back after that though LOL
A few examples of things that I love from these games include the world design, the music, and the character Lillie. There were so many quality of life improvements and the game is just so cozy. some may call it. revisionism that this game is getting love now, but I call it aging like fine wine.
You're so right that generations 8 and 9 are worse. nine was the absolute worst. couldn't even bring myself to play it.
overall, thank you for being happy that I enjoyed this game even if you perceive it to be slop. I'm happy to agree to disagree with polite people in this fandom. I know a lot of people have your perspective too, so I'm not going to call you crazy or something. let's just hope this new gen is going to make us both happy :D

text:
They've already done birds of all three starter types already, via Rowlet, Torchic, and Piplup. So another bird was bound to repeat a type. Same goes for lizards- we had Charmander, Treeko, and Sobble (and others open to interpretation).
That said, making the canine Fire again was a choice. We already had a fire canine starter in Fennekin. And then there's Growlithe, and Entei I guess, and although not Fire-type, Dachsbun is Fire-affiliated. This is like the Fire/Fighting controversy all over again. They seem to love repeating themes with Fire Pokemon.
So, if anything, you should be more annoyed at the Fire canine.

text:
I don’t think it should have to be necessarily “dark” themed, but it needs to still have the mysterious and strength personified aspect to them. Much like Lugia and HoOh (especially since the sea and the sky are legitimately related, if not attached/tied, to waves and wind).

Pokemon 2000 did an incredible job of making Lugia appear just slightly scary because of the almost folklore historical legends they told about it (along with sea serpent quick teaser moments hinting at its appearance with that hauntingly beautiful song). The final reveal made viewers awestruck because it showed that Lugia was truly a benevolent yet numinous Pokemon. It still was creating massive weather storms, changing currents throughout the entire ocean, and midair whirlpools while somehow equally holding its own battling 3 other legendary Pokemon!

Keeping that allure of how exotic/rare, powerful/potentially dangerous, and realization that this isn’t a Pokemon that’s meant to be caught because it’s a force of nature makes legendary Pokemon truly standout. I think Pokemon has lost that original and important impactful symbolism ever since diamond/pearl ended.

While I believe some legendary Pokemon shouldn’t be cute and have their own place in that world with darker undertones and natural evil morality, I agree these ones don’t have to be “dark” Pokemon. But I sure hope they return to their roots of what made Lugia and HoOh so unique, mysterious, and dominating forces in the Pokemon universe!
